mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, discount-rate pressure stays muted and equity risk premiums reprice higher, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-rate periods do not deliver higher 20-day forward returns than non-triggered periods, or if inflation acceleration dominates and SPX underperforms despite FEDFUNDS below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE indicates improving household income and lower recession probability, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher after UNRATE declines versus flat or rising UNRATE periods, or if the sign relationship flips in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger end-demand and housing-linked spending, which improves revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to outperform single-series or unconditional benchmarks for 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not positive in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL reflects improving real activity without an inflation shock, which raises expected corporate output and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises < 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if this high-real-low-inflation setup does not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative more often than positive under the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions and reduces the present value of future cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 50 bps over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not lower after 50 bp three-month hikes than after stable policy periods, or if hikes are followed by positive returns in a majority of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in GDPC1 signals aggregate demand contraction and earnings downgrades, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls on the latest quarterly print versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are not followed by lower 60-day SPX returns than quarters with positive GDP growth, or if the signal loses directional accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor slowdown, rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS implies weakening income growth and higher recession odds, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months while PAYEMS growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration trigger does not underperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rebounds after a prior decline, the market reads it as a demand stabilization signal that improves near-term earnings visibility and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises after two consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS rebound episodes do not produce higher 5-day SPX returns than other months, or if the average return remains non-positive across rebound triggers.
